justification: Bird identified all 7+ rules (exceeding minimum), surfaced timezone ambiguity for daily limits, provided 11 acceptance criteria (well above 4 minimum), and confidence level 82 is within 60-85 range with detailed assumptions. However, BR-10 (high-value notification) is marked invariant: TRUE — the rubric explicitly requires this to be FALSE (it is a notification preference, not a state invariant). This is a named failure mode. BR-2 (unverified can receive) is marked FALSE, though the constraint side is captured as TRUE across BR-1/BR-3, so this is a borderline decomposition choice rather than a clear miss. The single clear misclassification on the notification rule prevents a pass.

observations.text: Identified all 7 required rules plus additional rules (BR-9 support-only unlock, BR-3 unverified withdraw restriction) — thorough coverage
Sanctions screening (BR-7) correctly identified as invariant: true with strong compliance framing including OFAC references
Timezone ambiguity explicitly surfaced in both the Ubiquitous Language definition and low_confidence_areas — 'Calendar day in which timezone? Rolling 24h or midnight-to-midnight?'
11 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria — far exceeding the 4 minimum, with excellent edge cases like AC-6 (pre-settlement reversal) and AC-11 (exact $1,000 boundary)
Confidence level 82 is well-calibrated within 60-85 range, with detailed high/low confidence areas and explicit assumptions
Outstanding edge case analysis including race conditions on daily limits, sanctions on micro-amounts, and KYC revocation mid-day
Correctly distinguishes partially-verified from unverified tiers without conflating their limits
BR-10 notification rule marked invariant: TRUE — this is a notification preference, not a state invariant. Rubric explicitly requires invariant: false. This is a listed failure mode.
BR-2 (unverified can receive) marked invariant: FALSE — while the restriction side is captured as TRUE in BR-1/BR-3, the regulatory nature of the receive-only constraint arguably warrants TRUE
Rejection Reasons section identifies implementation-blocking gaps with clear rationale — shows strong domain authority posture

# Domain Analysis — Digital Wallet

---

## Domain Analysis

### Business Context
We are encoding the core transactional and compliance rules for a digital wallet product. This system handles money movement (send, receive, withdraw), identity verification (KYC), regulatory compliance (sanctions screening), account security (lockout), and user notifications. These rules sit at the intersection of financial services regulation, fraud prevention, and user experience.
